Rea-Sixty — native REAPER driver for SSL UF8 / UC1 (no SSL 360° needed)


Hi all,

After roughly two months of USB-protocol reverse engineering I'm finally happy enough with this to share it. Rea-Sixty is a REAPER extension that drives the SSL UF8 + UC1 directly from REAPER, replacing SSL 360° on the host side.

You don't need SSL 360° running, you don't need a virtual MIDI port, and you don't need an SSL plug-in on every track for track colours — the surface gets driven natively from REAPER's track / FX / parameter data.


What it does

- Track colour on the UF8 scribble strips — every REAPER track shows its assigned colour, no per-track plug-in needed.
- Full 16-bit motorised faders with capacitive touch + a calibrated correction table so the values match the printed scale (or whatever your SSL plug-in expects).
- UC1 follows the focused REAPER track and drives Channel Strip 2 / Bus Compressor 2 / 4K B/E/G / 360° Link.
- UC1 GR meter driven from the SSL plug-in itself via REAPER's PreSonus VST3 GainReduction_dB host-extension. Works for ReaComp / FabFilter / any plug-in implementing it.
- FX Learn — teach any third-party plug-in (VST/VST3/AU/JS) to behave as a virtual Channel-Strip or Bus-Comp, or just as 8 mapped UF8 strips. Survives reorders + renames via FX-GUID identity.
- Selection Sets — 8 slots, Snapshot or REAPER-Group, with optional auto-arming for automation writing.
- Parameter Groups — twist a knob on one track and the same parameter moves on every member track.
- Bindings system — every button, modifier combo (Shift/Cmd/Ctrl), and long-press is rebindable. Includes a REAPER Action picker so the surface can fire any REAPER action.
- REC + RME integration — works with my TotalReaper bridge to drive RME preamps (gain / 48V / pad / phase / AutoLevel) directly from the UF8 strips.
- Cross-platform — macOS (Apple Silicon, notarised), Windows (x64), Linux (x86_64).


How we built it

This is the part that took the time. SSL's "Plug-in Mixer" protocol is vendor-specific USB — completely undocumented and not MIDI. So:

- USB captures with Wireshark + USBPcap on Windows while SSL 360° was driving the device. Hundreds of pcaps, each labelled with what we triggered (a fader move, a colour change, a knob touch, a layer switch).
- For every frame: idle baseline vs trigger-event diffs. The novel bytes are the ones the trigger emitted.
- Decoded everything byte by byte — knob IDs, button IDs, LCD zone addressing, colour palette indices, motorised fader pitch-bend, the BC VU motor commands, the 3-state button LEDs, the LCD scribble strip layout, the central LCD overlays.

SSL ACP Support replied last week — no objections to the open-source project, but they won't share protocol docs. So firmware-update breakage is on me to chase; the protocol stays self-decoded.


Install

Via ReaPack (recommended):

Extensions → ReaPack → Manage repositories → Import/export → Import repositories
Paste: https://github.com/acklin83/reaper-scripts/raw/main/index.xml
Then Browse packages → Rea-Sixty → Install. Restart REAPER, then Preferences → Control/OSC/Web → Add → Rea-Sixty.

First-run setup:

- Windows: Settings → About → "Install UF8/UC1 WinUSB driver" (one UAC prompt).
- Linux: Settings → About → "Install Linux udev rule" (one pkexec prompt). Plug UF8 and UC1 into separate PC USB ports — daisy-chaining triggers xhci_hcd port-cycling on Linux 6.17.
- macOS: nothing extra. IOKit already lets libusb claim the device.

Manual install: download from https://github.com/acklin83/Rea-Sixty/releases — Mac dylibs are Developer-ID signed + Apple-notarised, Windows DLLs drop into %APPDATA%\REAPER\UserPlugins, Linux tarball ships the udev rule.

SSL 360° must not be running when REAPER starts — it claims the UF8/UC1 vendor interface exclusively. Quit it and you're good.


Caveats / current state

- This is v0.1.2 — works on my rig daily for the last few weeks, but you may hit edge cases. Open an issue on GitHub.
- One known macOS quirk: occasional mid-session USB disconnect that needs a physical replug to recover. Diagnosed as a host-side USB stack condition; libusb can't escape it. Working on it.
- ReaImGui is a soft requirement — without it the on-screen Settings window stays empty but hardware control still works. Install via ReaPack like everything else.


Manual + docs

Full user manual (PDF) is attached to the v0.1.2 GitHub release:
https://github.com/acklin83/Rea-Sixty/releases/tag/v0.1.2

Source on GitHub: https://github.com/acklin83/Rea-Sixty

Happy to answer questions in this thread. If anyone with a different REAPER setup hits something weird, I'd like to know.

Best,
Frank

(Stoersender Studio, Switzerland — https://www.stoersender-studio.ch)
